title: RFC: "podman image prune" seems to consider any unnamed images dangling
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

`podman image prune` removes intermediate build images, while `docker image prune` keeps them, allowing incremental work with Dockerfiles.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create an example Dockerfile:

```
[root@ferrum test]# cat Dockerfile
FROM debian:buster
RUN echo x > /root/x
RUN echo z > /root/z
```

2. Uses cache (it was previously built):

```
# podman build . -t test
STEP 1: FROM debian:buster
STEP 2: RUN echo x > /root/x
--> Using cache 5e4e46dfdae3ff075110690c56a6834be15040fadff256ea64e8fa7c28918bdc
STEP 3: RUN echo z > /root/z
--> Using cache 4d3fb1cdc16fbbb1372197c9a87bb1322b8d40004439d57e33c69d18dac732b5
STEP 4: COMMIT test
--> 4d3fb1cdc16
4d3fb1cdc16fbbb1372197c9a87bb1322b8d40004439d57e33c69d18dac732b5
```

3. Prune removes intermediate images

```
# podman image prune -f
d46586d389f0895e1a80cef0677e3f4419b35b325475f43d1bdab8b230e59d78
5e4e46dfdae3ff075110690c56a6834be15040fadff256ea64e8fa7c28918bdc
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Cache is lost:
```
[root@ferrum test]# podman build . -t test
STEP 1: FROM debian:buster
STEP 2: RUN echo x > /root/x
--> e272e1b232a
STEP 3: RUN echo z > /root/z
STEP 4: COMMIT test
--> 5886e10c886
5886e10c886c8bf1ecdeaa9229241420f9483b4a1ccf004394e352370316cf55
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
[root@ferrum test]# docker build . -t test
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Step 1/3 : FROM debian:buster
 ---> 971452c94376
Step 2/3 : RUN echo x > /root/x
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 57ae0b0baba0
Step 3/3 : RUN echo z > /root/z
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 65e23c2ea9b6
Successfully built 65e23c2ea9b6
Successfully tagged test:latest
```

```
[root@ferrum test]# docker image prune -f
Total reclaimed space: 0B
```

```
[root@ferrum test]# docker build . -t test
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Step 1/3 : FROM debian:buster
 ---> 971452c94376
Step 2/3 : RUN echo x > /root/x
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 57ae0b0baba0
Step 3/3 : RUN echo z > /root/z
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 65e23c2ea9b6
Successfully built 65e23c2ea9b6
Successfully tagged test:latest
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Docker way allows having a Dockerfile of, let's say, 30 steps (which take a day to build), and any prune command keeps every stage in the cache. This makes it possible to add a couple of stages to Dockerfile and rebuild it using cache, unless you explicitly pass `--no-cache` flag. Podman's prune command invalidates the cache for all the Dockerfiles you ever built, forcing you to rebuild all of them from scratch. While that might be desired for saving space in production environments, this is really uncomfortable for "intense Dockerfile development".

Basically, `docker image prune` does not remove any images which have dependent tagged images.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.8.1-dev
```

(was built from sources from master branch)

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: 3d48940927b1133e2bfd1c3accba84345cc56ee6
  go version: go1.12.17
  podman version: 1.8.1-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.14.2
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/local/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.12-dev, commit: 9f3f7842ed666feef174517c391553c3bc80a607'
  Distribution:
    distribution: altlinux
    version: "8.2"
  MemFree: 9637818368
  MemTotal: 16744488960
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-alt7.git0a012df
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: 0a012df867a2d525f62a146d8ebdf2e6ab8a5ad5
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 16868438016
  SwapTotal: 16868438016
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 12
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: ferrum.localdomain
  kernel: 4.19.33-un-def-alt0.M80P.1
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 1h 17m 29.68s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: Unknown
      Version: |-
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.7.7
        FUSE library version 3.4.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.27
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 10
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

Not installed as a package.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
